author: @AI_Storytelling_now
text: What kind of time frame does the hemp-plastic rot in? We talking months or decades? A solid century would be ideal I think. That way you can make somewhat meaningful things out of it that will last a generation or two but not stick around forever, forever.

author: @Garda.Parasailing
text: Hi Roberti, I want to write my graduation thesis about hemp plastic. I would like to write a business plan about a company that basically transform hemp into plastic, I am really interested in the process, if you can help me. It's a project that I would like to actually undertake once I graduated.

author: @RWBHere
text:
Thanks Robert; that's a huge improvement. The noise cancelling is still over-processing the audio a little, but it's perfectly intelligible. (Can it be turned off easily?)
That hemp plastic is very interesting, and it has scope for much experimentation. Best is that the material is 100% biodegradable / digestible.

author: @trailkeeper

author: @sarchlalaith8836
text: I'd like to see an inch thick plate shot with 7.62mm and see how it holds up

replies.author: @thaynemackey3188
replies.text: blended it with sheep wool and kevlar....shuts down the AK47 at 40 yards.

author: @michaelinamoment
text: Any thoughts of a preservative being added to elongate the erosion time? it being  biodegradable is good but two weeks may be too short of time.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: there are loads of things you could add - clove oil is good believe it or not

author: @olivierroy1301
text: Awesome. Single use gadgets should be made out of this.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's what I think and stuff we don't even think about that we make from plastic for some weird reason - like door handles, light switch cover plates, the feet on so many things and the knobs we use to tighten things - I have an exercise bike and there are 3 large plastic knobs on it and four feet - how many hundreds of thousands of those were made - sit inside and get put on the rubbish heap - when you look around there is just a ton of non critical uses this could be put to.

replies.author: @olivierroy1301
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I wasn't expecting those examples. It must be very strong.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @olivierroy1301 it is mate - we made an earlier trial version a couple of years ago and it was bullet proof - we shot an ak47 at it lol

replies.author: @olivierroy1301
replies.text: @whitemanbrownworld hahahaha!

author: @gcewing

author: @paulwedlock9788
text:
As Henry Ford made a car from hemp based plastic ( Sisal, hemp & resin) could an alternative fiber mat material fill that niche?
Or disposal super cap  batteries?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I suspect so but not for caps mate - the water based environment and desire for them to rot would give caps made from this a very short life

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @reincarnation-milk
text: Cough cough... McDonalds drinking straws cough cough!!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: so let's not try eh? - let the world go to hell in a handbasket - much better that trying to do something is that the idea?

replies.author: @reincarnation-milk
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering No...it wasnt a snidy remark. My point was....Out there in consumer world, there is uproar because the replacement for the 'traditional' plastic McDonalds straw is cardboard because of the oceans becoming basically landfill! If someone could invent a straw that could A. Not turn to mush halfway through the heart attack inducing milkshake  AND  B.  Rot away a couple of weeks later, I recon a very, very, very large contract would be on the offing because weirdly enough....its a pretty large issue online and McDonalds are feeling the heat and are under MUCH pressure to return to the fish killing plastic straws. Honestly....this is a real thing!!!  Love you work, been watching for years.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @reincarnation-milk I am sorry  mate - i didn't get that - I apologise for being an ass - that is an awesome suggestion! - cheers

author: @HempysHub
text: Hello we are community of enthusiast you working in the industrial hemp industry we were wondering if we could use your video on our website and YouTube channel we will give you full credit and promote you?

author: @sonnyhayes689
text: Hey Rob... Still having so much fun. Toyota uses hemp plastic for some trim parts and converted soy bean oil as well for injection molding. More companies need to convert to using more renewables. Unfortunately the supply is not very stable and quality varies from supplier to supplier.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is unfortunate

author: @Hexafoose
text: Why don't they use hemp plastic for roofing material, or anything that should be built durable.  Like Henry Ford's first cars.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Seems like plastic waste pollution is largely single-use packaging.  It would be interesting to single-side coat a degradeable packaging material with a preservative so that it would degrade quickly once it is opened and the product is removed.  Also, I'm not sure that you should disregard development of a cellulose-reinforced composite material, it could be useful.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am not discounting them - i am saying this is not the same thing
